tickerText: [TENNIS] Queen's semi-final Saturday on Centre Court: Brandon Nakashima faces Francisco Cerundolo at 1:00pm after stunning top seed Alex de Minaur 7-5, 6-3; 2024 champion Tommy Paul meets Ugo Humbert at 2:00pm — Paul extended his Queen's unbeaten run to eight with a 6-3, 7-6(4) QF win over Davidovich Fokina while Humbert saved four match points in a two-day R16 epic before thrashing Rinky Hijikata 6-1, 6-2 • [SQUASH] PSA Tour Finals championship Saturday at Centquatre-Paris: Sivasangari Subramaniam — first Malaysian woman in a Tour Finals final since Nicol David — takes on world No. 1 Hania El Hammamy after stunning world champion Amina Orfi 8-11, 11-10, 11-8, 11-10; Paul Coll meets Diego Elias in the men's title decider after Coll beat Mohamad Zakaria and Elias advanced past Mostafa Asal • [BADMINTON] Macau Open semi-final Saturday at the East Asian Games Dome: Malaysia's Eogene Ewe stuns second seed Jason Teh 21-9, 21-19 for a maiden Super 300 semi-final; Ong/Carmen Ting, Low/Ng, Chia/Teo, and Hoo/Lai also through as the $370,000 BWF World Tour stop sets Sunday finals • [PICKLEBALL] Beijing Open semifinal Saturday at China's National Tennis Center — PPA Tour Asia's Olympic-venue debut moves into last-four matches across all pro divisions ahead of Championship Sunday medal matches June 21 on the Capital Diamond Court • [TABLE TENNIS] WTT Star Contender Ljubljana Day 5: Huang Youzheng/Chen Yi rally from 9-6 down to beat Togami/Odo 12-10 in mixed doubles QF and set a semi vs Lim/Shin; Lin Shidong falls 1-3 to Yukiya Uda; Britain's Tom Jarvis continues his run after a straight-games R64 upset of Anton Kallberg — main-draw action at Hala Tivoli through June 21

newsItems.title: Queen's Club Semi-Finals Set for Saturday
newsItems.excerpt: The HSBC Championships reach semi-final Saturday at The Queen's Club after a blockbuster Friday: Brandon Nakashima knocked out top seed Alex de Minaur 7-5, 6-3, Francisco Cerundolo edged British wildcard Arthur Fery in three sets, 2024 champion Tommy Paul beat Alejandro Davidovich Fokina 6-3, 7-6(4), and Ugo Humbert completed a double-duty day with a 6-1, 6-2 rout of Rinky Hijikata. Nakashima vs Cerundolo and Paul vs Humbert headline Centre Court from 1:00pm London time.

newsItems.title: Sivasangari Makes History at PSA Tour Finals
newsItems.excerpt: Malaysia's Sivasangari Subramaniam stunned world champion Amina Orfi 8-11, 11-10, 11-8, 11-10 in Paris to become the first Malaysian woman in a PSA Tour Finals title match since Nicol David, and will face world No. 1 Hania El Hammamy — who beat Fayrouz Aboelkheir in the other women's semi. Paul Coll and Diego Elias advanced to the men's championship decider at Centquatre-Paris on season-ending finals day.

newsItems.title: Eogene Ewe Sparks Malaysia's Macau Open Run
newsItems.excerpt: World No. 80 Eogene Ewe delivered the Macau Open's biggest upset on semi-final Saturday, beating second seed Jason Teh 21-9, 21-19 for his first BWF World Tour Super 300 semi-final at the East Asian Games Dome. Malaysia also advanced Ong Xin Yee/Carmen Ting in women's doubles, Low Hang Yee/Ng Eng Cheong and Chia Wei Jie/Teo Ee Yi in men's doubles, and Hoo Pang Ron/Lai Pei Jing in mixed doubles ahead of Sunday's $370,000 finals.

newsItems.title: Huang and Chen Rally Into Ljubljana Mixed Semis
newsItems.excerpt: China's Huang Youzheng and Chen Yi saved match point in a 3-2 mixed doubles quarter-final comeback over Japan's Shunsuke Togami and Miyuu Kihara at WTT Star Contender Ljubljana, winning the decider 12-10 after trailing 9-6. Chen contested five matches in one day as the $300,000 Star Contender reached its penultimate stage; second seed Lin Shidong was upset 1-3 by Yukiya Uda in men's singles round-of-32 play.
